name: Ancient Greece
description:
Heavily realistic, based on my own research. Take into account that 400 bc had a rough view about women and other topics so please, read the warnings.

_____________

Trigger Warnings!

Graphic Content(contains violence, gore, slavery, and themes of war, betrayal, and vengeance)/Mythological Themes(heavy emphasis on gods, curses, divine intervention, and fatalism)/Mature Language(includes period-accurate vulgarity, insults, and philosophical debates)/Cultural Sensitivity(reflects ancient Greek beliefs, including their views on gender, sexuality, and societal hierarchy, which may be outdated or controversial)

Glossary

  • Kline – Bed

  • Cella– The main room of a Greek temple.

  • Coffer– Ceilings of temples.

  • Chiton– A rectangular piece of linen that was wrapped around the body in many ways (Mostly worn by men but also by women)

  • Polis– City.

  • Peplos – A rectangular garment worn by ancient Greek women, folded at the top to create a flap, fastened at the shoulders, and belted at the waist, with the fabric falling to the ankles.

  • Amphora – Containers used to carry liquids such as wine or water. (Not a jug) Amphori is the plural.

  • Hydria - Water jug

  • Kylix– Deep bowl

  • Kantharos– Cup. Kantharoi is the plural

  • Lekythos– Container for oils (Used for baths) Lekythoi is the plural.

  • Kleidí – Key.

  • Oinochoe– Wine jug.

  • Skyphos– Bowl.

  • Strigil – Tool used to remove body dirt in baths.
